data.title: As an engineering team lead, I want T4 kudos trigger to award points for measurable reduction in duplicate Python blocks per session
data.description:
As an engineering team lead, I need T4 (duplicate Python block reduction) kudos trigger to award points for measurable deduplication work so that systematic refactoring is explicitly incentivized.

DONE DEFINITIONS:
- T4 integrated as function in commit-validate.sh (same integration point as Epic 1 T-series triggers)
- T4: identify Python files in git diff $(git merge-base HEAD main) HEAD --name-only (deleted files excluded; Python files only, matched by .py extension)
- Pre-session state: git show <snapshot.merge_base>:<path> for each file; if git show fails, skip that file with warning
- Python AST subtree hashing per S1 spike findings (0ecc-9ce1): hashlib.sha256(ast.dump(node).encode()); minimum subtree size = 5 statements
- Duplicate count = number of (hash, count) pairs where count >= 2 across all changed Python files
- Kudos awarded when post-commit duplicate count < pre-session duplicate count (reduction >= 1)
- Graceful degradation: Python unavailable (command -v python3) → skip with warning; merge-base unavailable → skip with warning; file read error → skip that file; all skips are non-blocking (fail-open)
- Snapshot tests map empty → T4 still evaluates (T4 does not require snapshot test data; uses snapshot.merge_base only for git show)
- Unit tests cover: no Python files changed, reduction detected (award), no reduction (no award), Python absent (skip), merge-base absent (skip)

CONSIDERATIONS:
- [Blocker] L depends on Epic 1 Story A (b41b-0369) for ledger infrastructure
- [Blocker] L depends on Story J (ef94-8be1) for snapshot.merge_base field
- [Blocker] L depends on Spike S1 (0ecc-9ce1) for AST subtree hashing approach
- [Scope] deleted files excluded — removing a file full of duplicates does not earn T4 kudos; extracting duplicated code into a shared utility (adds new file, modifies existing) DOES count
